mission.title: Install and Configure Notary Project on Kubernetes
mission.description: Notation is a CLI tool designed to sign and verify artifacts in the OCI registry ecosystem, enhancing security similar to git commit signatures. Installing Notation allows you to ensure the integrity and authenticity of your container images.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for Notation to run in. This helps to isolate the resources for better management.

steps.title: Apply the Notation Deployment
steps.description: Deploy the Notation application using the provided Kubernetes manifests from the GitHub repository.

steps.title: Apply the Notation Service
steps.description: Expose the Notation application using a Service resource.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check if the Notation pods are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Notation will show the Notation pod in a running state when you check with `kubectl get pods`. You will also be able to access the Notation service locally through port 8080.

uninstall.title: Remove Notation Resources
uninstall.description: Delete the Notation deployment and service along with the namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Notation
upgrade.description: To upgrade Notation, fetch the latest version of the manifests and apply them again.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Running
troubleshooting.description: If the Notation pod is not in a Running state, check the pod logs for errors using: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n notation`.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: If the service is not accessible, ensure that the service type is correctly set (e.g., NodePort or LoadBalancer) and check the service configuration with: `kubectl describe service notation -n notation`.

troubleshooting.title: Namespace Issues
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter issues related to the namespace, ensure that the namespace was created successfully and is active by running: `kubectl get namespaces`.

prerequisites.description: Ensure that you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
